Enhancements for the workflow restart screen      Adding fields (not sure what is the best fields for this purpose yet) to show what grant amount or GSR the workflow queue is related to. This would be helpful for users to identify the workflow queue he/she wants to restart. Currently on the Workflow Restart screen, there are multiple workflow queues for each grant, in some cases there could be too many, and users have trouble to find the workflow queue. For example Req#121524 have over 100 workflow queues on the screen (refer to screenshot below). As a result, 18 TDL items were recreated created because wrong workflow queues were restarted by mistake.   2. Display only the latest workflow queue of each workflow for each grant amount or grant. For example, there are total 6 workflow queues for Grant Amount 133948 (see highlighted workflow queue below). Instead of displaying all the 6 workflow queues, displaying ONLY the workflow queue 52376 (latest for Fund Request – V1) and 53823 (latest for GAP - Grant Amount Level - V1) will help to avoid users restarting old workflow queues and also make it easier to find the needed workflow queue.

New Process for Diversion/Verification data entry for ER Report         I've received the go-ahead from Legal Services to revise the Diversion/Verification data entry process for the ER Report.  Grants Managers will (as always) review their ER grants with the Primary Grantmaker.  They will prepare a memo confirming that this review has been done and either stating that there were no suspected diversions or listing any.  They will manually enter any suspected diversions (rarely more than one a year -- if that).  When that has been done, we would like all 2013 ER records to have the default entries for Diversion/Verification globally changed from Not Determined" to "No Diversion"/"Not Verified." This will probably be sometime in February or early March; but I'd like us to be able to test it by the first week in February."

Active Grantees Contact Info Report does not work properly             This report should list all grantees with at least one active grant as of a selected run date. It does not. I've pinpointed a few of the problems:     1) The report requires start date and end date as parameters, but it's not clear what should be entered. By experimentation I determined that the report will not include any grantee where the active grant’s ORIGINAL end date is earlier than the selected end date, even if the current end date is in the future. Furthermore, it needs to be determined whether for the Office of Communications' purposes an active grant is a grant whose status is approved, a grant that has not yet reached its end date, or a grant that has not yet been closed (for example, an expired grant with capital equipment reports pending).   2) The report also omits grants that do not have an end date -- consequently, Investees where the PRI has not yet closed, grantees with an equity grant associated with a PRI that has not yet closed, and grantees with grants awaiting government approval are omitted from the list.   It would probably be helpful to add an additional parameter: Country, with the option of choosing a single country, all countries, or a selection of countries.       Reference Ticket : 607776   I would recommend checking with the Office of Communications regarding the priority to be assigned fixing this report. When they need it, it is usually right away or close to it.
